reasoning: Polymarket has made significant and definitive steps toward launching a US-regulated Designated Contract Market (DCM) in 2025. Key factors overwhelmingly favoring a launch by December 31, 2025 include: 1) The acquisition of QCX, an existing CFTC-licensed DCM. 2) A CFTC no-action letter issued in September 2025, which the CEO referred to as the 'green light' to launch. 3) Polymarket US began self-certifying event contracts in early October 2025. 4) A monumental $2 billion investment from the Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) announced in October 2025, providing massive operational and institutional momentum. 5) A waitlist was already live on their website in October 2025. The only identified potential delays are minor (FOIA requests from third parties and temporary government shutdowns). Even if the launch is slightly delayed past the initially slated early October dates, over two months remain in 2025 for at least one 'real-money trade' to be publicly placed on the platform, which is a low hurdle to clear for resolution.
